title: Silent Letters and Weak Words
learningGoal: The learner will accurately produce the weak form of 'can' (/kən/) versus the negative 'can't' (/kænt/ or /kɑːnt/), and suppress the phonological realization of the 'L' in 'should' (/ʃʊd/).
instructions: Modals have a few tricky sounds! First, NEVER say the 'L' in the word 'Should'. It is completely silent. Second, the word 'Can' is usually spoken very weakly and quickly like 'k'n', so that the real action verb sounds louder!

contentItems.text: SH-UH-D. Do not put an 'L' sound in Should! Your tongue should not touch the top of your mouth for the L.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The L is completely silent! Rhymes with 'good' or 'wood'. Sh-uh-d.
Two separate words. Still no L! 'SHUD NOT'.
Fast 'can', heavy 'Go'. It sounds like 'I k'n GO'. Native speakers squish 'can'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Stressing 'CAN' heavily, making it sound robotic.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Hard 'T' stop at the end! KAA-NT. (or CAN-T in American). It must have a 'T'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Forgetting the 'T', making it sound exactly like positive 'I can go'.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Heavy emphasis on MUST. It is a powerful word.
VERY tricky! The first 'T' is totally silent! Muss-int.
Clear 'T' on Must. Silent 'T' on listen!
Weak can + Strong read. She k'n READ.

expectedPronunciationText: Should Shouldn't I can go I can't go

feedbackRules.onPass: Awesome! You conquered the tricky silences. Your 'Should' perfectly dropped the L, and your negative 'Can't' had a great, clear 'T' sound!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! Two major rules to remember: NEVER wrap your tongue on the roof of your mouth for 'Should'. It is 'Shud'. And make sure your 'Can't' has a powerful 'T' at the end so people know you are saying NO!
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Watch out for 'Mustn't'. It is 'Muss-int'. Do not say the first T!

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: should should not shouldn't i can go i cannot go i can't go you must stop must not mustn't you shouldn't run we can help we can't help they must listen he shouldn't yell she can read
acceptedVariants: should should not shouldn't i can go i cannot go i cant go you must stop must not mustn't you shouldn't run we can help we cant help they must listen he shouldn't yell she can read
should should not shouldn't i can go i can not go i can't go you must stop must not mustn't you shouldn't run we can help we can't help they must listen he shouldn't yell she can read
adminCorrectAnswerSet.phoneticApproximation: Requires active suppression of /l/ realization in /ʃʊd/ and targeted articulation of voiceless alveolar block /t/ in negated modals to ensure semantic disambiguation between can/can't.

hintText: To make the correct 'Can't' sound, bite your teeth together sharply and spit a tiny bit of air at the very end to make the 'T' pop!
commonMistakes: Blurring 'Can' and 'Can't' together so listeners cannot tell if it is positive or negative.
